title: Vocabulary Duel — Ocean words
description: Vocabulary blueprint using the same Story Duel engine.
subtitle: Word bank + rubric + Ollama; heuristic uses substring_hints and keywords.
passage:
Word bank for this duel:
• abundant — more than enough; plenty
• fragile — easy to break or damage
• navigate — to find your way; to steer a course

Context: Coral reefs are abundant in warm, shallow water. Divers must navigate carefully around fragile sea fans.

rounds.title: Define: abundant
rounds.prompt: Explain what abundant means in your own words (one or two sentences).
rounds.rubric: Valid if the student conveys the idea of a large amount, plenty, or more than enough. Minor wording errors are OK.

rounds.title: Use: fragile
rounds.prompt: Write a sentence that uses fragile correctly.
rounds.rubric: Valid if fragile describes something that can break easily (object, relationship, peace, etc.). Reject if the word is used as a verb or with a nonsense meaning.

rounds.title: Context: navigate
rounds.prompt: In the passage, what does navigate suggest the divers are doing? Answer in a short phrase.
rounds.rubric: Valid if they connect navigate to finding a path, steering, moving carefully, or avoiding damage in the reef.
